compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
# Durable write path drops concurrent locked writes: background git auto-gc races the object DB

## Summary
Under heavy concurrent write load, the rebar durable write path (`event_append`) loses
committed writes. Writers holding the exclusive index lock fail their `git commit` with
`error: invalid object <sha> for '<...>.json' ... error: Error building trees` and the
write is silently dropped. The regression test
`tests/interfaces/store/test_enrich_drain_prune_concurrency.py::test_enrich_queue_prune_never_drops_concurrent_locked_writes`
catches it (e.g. `intended=250 persisted_on_disk=233 lost=17`), and it is **currently red on
`main` repo-wide** — it fails ~9 of the last ~12 `main` post-merge CI runs, including the CI
run of the very commit that tried to fix it (`485873652`, post-merge run `29593239791`,
`lost=17`). Because it fails Verified on unrelated changes, it is friction on every Gerrit
landing (mitigated today only by probabilistic `recheck`).

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run the integration tier under parallel xdist load, e.g.
   `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/interfaces/store/test_enrich_drain_prune_concurrency.py -q`
   repeatedly, or observe `main`'s post-merge `test` workflow across several pushes.
2. The test spawns `_WRITERS=5` writers × `_BURSTS=25` (`intended=250`) concurrent
   lock-serialized writes while the enrich-queue prune runs.
3. Intermittently (load-dependent), some writes raise the `invalid object / Error building
   trees` `CommandError` while holding the lock and are swallowed → `lost>0`.

**Expected:** every lock-acquiring write persists to disk; `swallowed_write_raises == 0` and
`persisted_on_disk == intended_total`.
**Actual:** under load a fraction of writes are lost (observed losses 17/26/94/146 across
runs/workers — the varying count is itself a signature of a load-dependent race).

## Root Cause (diagnosed)
- `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` (~L96–117) deliberately re-enabled **stock git background gc**
  on the tracker repo — `--unset gc.auto` + `gc.autoDetach=true` (commit `f63d72662`,
  "WU-1 — re-enable stock git gc (drop gc.auto=0 forcing)", 2026-06-18).
- Under this test's heavy parallel commit load the loose-object count crosses git's `gc.auto`
  threshold; git forks a **detached background `gc --auto`** that repacks/prunes loose objects
  **concurrently with the writers** — an object can vanish between `git add` and `git commit`.
- `485873652` added `_recover_from_invalid_object` (Mode D, `event_append.py:713`): on an
  `invalid object / error building trees` commit failure it runs `git read-tree HEAD`, re-adds
  the current write's path, and retries. This reduced the damage ~6–8× (146 → ~17) but does
  **not** cover the case where the missing object is **reachable from HEAD** (a committed
  object caught by the background repack): `read-tree HEAD` reinstates the broken reference, the
  retry commit fails identically, and the recovery is one-shot (no bounded loop / escalation).
  The failing logs show the **same object sha for the same path cascading across sequential
  bursts**, which is the HEAD-reachable signature.

## Fix Direction (candidate; needs maintainer judgment on the gc trade-off)
1. **Neutralize the trigger on the durable write path:** force `gc.auto=0` (or at minimum
   `gc.autoDetach=false`) on the tracker repo so a background repack cannot run concurrently
   with commits. This revisits the deliberate `f63d72662` decision — understand *why* WU-1
   re-enabled gc (loose-object growth? perf?) before reverting; a scoped
   "no auto-gc while the exclusive write lock is held" is preferable to a blanket revert.
2. **Harden the recovery to the HEAD-reachable case:** after `read-tree HEAD`, if the missing
   object is still referenced from HEAD, regenerate it from the intact worktree file
   (re-`git add` the committed path from disk) before retry; make `_recover_from_invalid_object`
   a **bounded loop** rather than one-shot.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root cause confirmed: a targeted repro demonstrates a background `gc --auto` (or manual
      `git gc` under load) is what prunes the object referenced by the failing commit.
- [ ] The durable write path no longer loses writes under the test's load: 
      `test_enrich_queue_prune_never_drops_concurrent_locked_writes` passes **50/50 consecutive
      runs locally** under xdist parallelism (currently flakes).
- [ ] `main` post-merge CI passes this test across **10 consecutive** pushes after the fix lands
      (was failing ~9/12).
- [ ] The chosen gc trade-off is documented (a code comment + the ticket) explaining the
      interaction between WU-1's gc re-enablement and the write path, so it is not silently
      re-reverted.
- [ ] No regression in the durable-write / enrich-drain unit + integration tiers
      (`make test` green).

## Notes / Provenance
Discovered while landing epic 6982's R6 change (`5dac`, Gerrit #760): #760 touches nothing in
`src/rebar/_store/*` yet failed this test on CI, while sibling #761 (superset runtime tree)
passed — i.e. a probabilistic store-layer race, not a defect in the plan-review changes. Full
diagnosis captured here so a maintainer can pick it up; not in epic 6982's plan-review scope.
compiled_state.title: Durable write path drops concurrent locked writes: background git auto-gc races object DB
